vocabulary.definition: a Japanese snack made from cooked rice; usually wrapped in seaweed paper

examples.text: Most children in Japan love eating rice balls.
examples.ko: 대부분의 일본 아이들은 주먹밥 먹는 것을 좋아한다.
examples.zh: 大部分的日本孩子都喜歡吃飯糰。

vocabulary.definition: a plant growing in the sea

examples.text: The piece of sushi is wrapped with seaweed.
examples.ko: 그 초밥 한 조각은 김으로 싸여있다.
examples.zh: 這塊壽司用海苔包裹著。

vocabulary.definition: a round dish on which food is served and eaten or food on a plate

examples.text: The chef is putting food on the plate.
examples.ko: 주방장이 접시 위에 음식을 담고 있습니다.
examples.zh: 大厨正在把食物装盘。

examples.text: I had a delicious plate of pasta for lunch today.
examples.zh: 我今天中午吃了一盘美味的意大利面条。

vocabulary.definition: part of the beliefs, way of life, etc. of a particular culture that has existed for a long time

examples.text: They're both dressed in traditional costumes.
examples.ko: 그들은 둘 다 전통 의상을 입고 있다.
examples.zh: 她们都穿着传统服饰。

examples.text: I love traditional Japanese music.
examples.zh: 我喜欢传统的日本音乐。

vocabulary.definition: 3; the number after two

examples.text: My baby is three months old.
examples.zh: 我的小孩有三个月大了。

examples.text: I'd like to rent a car for three days.
examples.zh: 我想租一辆车，租期三天。

vocabulary.definition: to place food in the mouth, then chew and swallow it

examples.text: I like to eat a big breakfast.
examples.ko: 나는 아침을 많이 먹는 것을 좋아합니다.
examples.zh: 我早餐喜欢吃很多。

examples.text: She can't decide what to eat.
examples.zh: 她决定不了吃什么。

vocabulary.definition: to be built or created from (some material or ingredient)

examples.text: This table is made of wood.
examples.zh: 這張桌子是木製的。

lessonTitle: A Day in the Life of a Business Person
prompt: Describe the picture below. Check the Helpful Vocabulary section if you need help.

vocabulary.definition: a structure with walls, a roof, and windows

examples.text: This building has a very original design.
examples.ko: 이 건물은 매우 독창적인 디자인을 가지고 있다.
examples.zh: 这幢建筑有着许多独创的设计。

examples.text: That building is the post office.
examples.zh: 那栋建筑是邮局。

vocabulary.definition: the space above the earth's surface

examples.text: The sky is very clear today.
examples.zh: 今天天空非常晴朗。

examples.text: The sky was beautiful last night at sunset.
examples.zh: 昨天日落时分的天空非常美丽。

vocabulary.definition: thick structures used to enclose spaces or divide rooms in buildings

examples.text: The castle is protected by high walls.
examples.ko: 높은 벽이 그 성을 보호하고 있습니다.
examples.zh: 城堡四面筑有高墙防护。

examples.text: She is painting the wall blue.
examples.zh: 她正在把墙面刷成蓝色。

vocabulary.definition: a type of glass that reflects images

examples.text: She's looking at herself in the mirror.
examples.ko: 그녀는 거울에 비친 자신을 바라보고 있습니다.
examples.zh: 她正在照镜子。

examples.text: She's putting on her lipstick in front of the mirror.
examples.ko: 그녀는 거울 앞에서 립스틱을 바르고 있습니다.
examples.zh: 她在镜子前涂她的唇膏。

vocabulary.definition: very large

examples.text: They're thinking of buying a huge house.
examples.ko: 그들은 큰 집을 살 생각을 하고 있습니다.
examples.zh: 他们正想买一所大房子。

examples.text: That's a huge sandwich.
examples.zh: 这个三明治真大。

vocabulary.definition: having the color of the sky

examples.text: You have beautiful blue eyes.
examples.ko: 당신은 아름다운 파란색 눈을 가지고 계십니다.
examples.zh: 你有着漂亮的蓝色眼睛。

examples.text: We've had blue skies all week.
examples.ko: 이번 주 내내 파란색 하늘이 보였습니다.
examples.zh: 整个周天都是蓝的。

vocabulary.definition: (of a mirror or other shiny surface) to show an image

examples.text: A mirror reflects an image so that it appears in reverse.
examples.ko: 거울은 그것이 거꾸로 나타나도록 한 상을 비춘다.
examples.zh: 镜面会反射图像，所以看起来方向是相反的。

vocabulary.definition: a white or gray mass in the sky that is made of vapor

examples.text: There are a lot of clouds in the sky today.
examples.zh: 今天天上有很多云。

examples.text: The sun is behind the clouds.
examples.zh: 太阳被云遮住了。

prompt: Describe the picture below. Check the Helpful Vocabulary section if you need help.

vocabulary.definition: a female parent

examples.text: She's a new mother.
examples.zh: 她刚做了妈妈。

examples.text: She's going to be a mother soon.
examples.zh: 她快做妈妈了。

vocabulary.definition: a girl or woman in relation to her parents

examples.text: You and your daughter really look alike.
examples.zh: 你和你女儿长得很像。

examples.text: My daughter is a little shy.
examples.zh: 我的女儿有点害羞。

vocabulary.definition: a room or area where food is kept and cooked

examples.text: I want to live in a house with a big kitchen.
examples.ko: 나는 큰 부엌이 있는 집에서 살고 싶다.
examples.zh: 我想住在一个有大厨房的房子里。

examples.text: My kids really like playing in the kitchen.
examples.ko: 나의 아이들은 주방에서 노는 것을 정말 좋아한다.
examples.zh: 我的孩子们真喜欢在厨房里玩。

vocabulary.definition: a part of a plant that can be eaten, such as a potato, cabbage, onion, etc.

examples.text: Eating lots of fruit and vegetables is good for you.
examples.ko: 과일과 채소를 많이 먹으면 몸에 좋습니다.
examples.zh: 多吃蔬菜水果有利于你的健康。

examples.text: We had a nice meal of steak and vegetables last night.
examples.ko: 어제저녁에 스테이크와 채소의 좋은 식사를 했다.
examples.zh: 昨晚我们吃了牛排和蔬菜，真是一顿美餐。

vocabulary.definition: a thick liquid made from plants or animals, used in cooking
jewelry that can be put on a finger

examples.text: He gave me a beautiful diamond ring.
examples.ko: 그가 내게 아름다운 다이아몬드 반지를 주었다.
examples.zh: 他送给我一枚漂亮的钻石戒指。

examples.text: We just bought our wedding rings.
examples.zh: 我们刚刚买了结婚戒指。

vocabulary.definition: including a lot of different colors

examples.text: A colorful painting of lotus flowers was on display.
examples.zh: 有一幅色彩繽紛的荷花圖畫展出。

vocabulary.definition: to prepare food by mixing, combining, and heating ingredients

examples.text: I cook meals for my family most nights.
examples.ko: 나는 거의 매일 밤 내 가족을 위해 식사를 요리한다.
examples.zh: 大多数晚上我会为家人做饭。

examples.text: They are learning how to cook.
examples.zh: 他们在学习烹饪。

vocabulary.definition: to transfer knowledge; to instruct

examples.text: He's teaching the children how to swim.
examples.ko: 그는 아이들에게 수영하는 방법을 가르치고 있습니다.
examples.zh: 他在教孩子们怎样游泳。

examples.text: She is teaching her daughter how to read.
examples.ko: 그녀는 딸에게 글 읽는 법을 가르치고 있습니다.
examples.zh: 她在教女儿怎样阅读。

vocabulary.definition: to make physical contact with someone or something; to place one's hand on someone or something

examples.text: The baby reached out and touched his finger.
examples.ko: 아기가 손을 뻗어 그의 손가락을 만졌습니다.
examples.zh: 小宝宝伸手触摸他的手指。

examples.text: You can type by touching keys on the screen.
examples.ko: 화면의 키를 눌러서 입력할 수 있습니다.
examples.zh: 你可以轻触屏幕上的键进行输入。

vocabulary.definition: to make a liquid flow into or onto something

examples.text: Can you pour me another glass of wine?
examples.ko: 와인 한 잔 더 따라 주시겠습니까?
examples.zh: 能再为我倒杯酒吗？

examples.text: He's pouring some milk on his cereal.
examples.zh: 他正把牛奶倒到燕麦片上。

vocabulary.definition: to raise the corners of one's mouth in order to show happiness or pleasure

examples.text: He's smiling at the camera.
examples.ko: 그는 카메라를 보고 미소를 짓고 있습니다.
examples.zh: 他正对着镜头笑呢。

examples.text: You look so happy when you smile.
examples.ko: 당신은 웃을 때 아주 행복해 보여요.
examples.zh: 你笑的时候看起来很开心。

vocabulary.definition: a small furry animal often kept as a pet, related to tigers, lions, etc.

examples.text: Humans began keeping cats as pets to hunt rats and mice.
examples.ko: 사람들은 쥐를 잡기 위해 고양이를 애완동물로 키우기 시작했습니다.

vocabulary.definition: the blossoming part of a plant; the part of a plant that has petals

examples.text: I went shopping for flowers in the morning.
examples.zh: 我早上去买了花。

examples.text: He gave me some nice flowers.
examples.zh: 他送给我一些漂亮的花。

vocabulary.definition: the bottom part of the leg that one stands on

examples.text: I like the feel of sand on my feet.
examples.ko: 나의 발에 닿는 모래의 느낌이 좋다.

vocabulary.definition: an object used for entering and exiting a house, building, car, etc.

examples.zh: 她正在锁门。

vocabulary.definition: person who is female and an adult

examples.zh: 她是一位聪明的年轻女人。

examples.text: There are many women at this bar.
examples.zh: 这个酒吧里有很多女人。

vocabulary.definition: having the color of the sun or lemons

examples.zh: 我丈夫给我买了一打黄玫瑰。

examples.text: She's wearing a yellow kimono.
examples.zh: 她穿着一套黄色的和服。

vocabulary.definition: having the color of blood, tomatoes, fire, etc.

examples.text: She's wearing a red dress.
examples.zh: 她穿着一件红色的衣服。

examples.text: I have a red sports car.
examples.zh: 我有一辆红色的跑车。

vocabulary.definition: made or produced from wood

examples.zh: 这扇木门是关着的。

examples.text: We bought a pair of wooden chairs for our deck.
examples.ko: 우리는 우리의 테라스에 둘 나무로 된 의자 한 쌍을 구매했다.

vocabulary.definition: to carry something somewhere; to take someone somewhere

examples.zh: 服务员端来了我们的鸡尾酒。

examples.text: My dog brought me the newspaper.
examples.zh: 我的狗狗帮我拿来了报纸。

vocabulary.definition: be on your feet and not move

examples.text: He's standing at the top of the mountain.

examples.text: They're standing in a long line.
examples.zh: 他们排成一条长队。

vocabulary.definition: to use vision to focus, or turn one's eyes to something

examples.text: What are you looking at?

vocabulary.definition: to get into a place

examples.text: He entered the house through the front door.
examples.zh: 他从前门进入了房间。

examples.text: He just entered the room.
examples.zh: 他刚刚才进入房间。

vocabulary.definition: to have in one's hands or arms

examples.ko: 잠깐 동안 이 상자 좀 붙잡아 주시겠습니까?
examples.zh: 你可以把这些盒子抱一会儿吗？

examples.text: The mother is holding her baby.
examples.zh: 妈妈抱着婴儿。

vocabulary.definition: the part of the body at the end of the arm that includes the palm, fingers, and thumb

examples.text: She has paint on her hands.
examples.ko: 그녀는 그녀의 손에 페인트를 묻히고 있다.
examples.zh: 她的手上涂了颜料。

examples.text: He writes with his left hand.
examples.zh: 他用左手写字。

vocabulary.definition: the act of pressing and rubbing the muscles of the body with the hands in order to relieve stress or pain

examples.text: Getting a massage feels good.
examples.zh: 做按摩让人感觉很舒服。

examples.text: At that spa, you can get a great massage at a very good price.
examples.ko: 스파에서, 아주 좋은 가격에 훌륭한 마사지를 받을 수 있다.
examples.zh: 在那間水療中心可以得到實惠又不錯的按摩。

vocabulary.definition: a soft cloth used for drying the body or other objects

examples.text: There are clean towels on the bed.
examples.zh: 床上有干净的毛巾。

examples.text: A cold towel might help her temperature to drop.
examples.ko: 그녀의 체온을 내리는 데 차가운 수건이 도움이 될 것입니다.
examples.zh: 一条冰毛巾有助于降低她的体温。

vocabulary.definition: the upper part of the body containing the brain

examples.text: My head hurts.
examples.zh: 我头疼。

examples.text: He's wearing a crown on his head.
examples.zh: 他头戴着一顶王冠。

vocabulary.definition: the organ that allows animals and humans to see

examples.text: I can only see her eyes.
examples.zh: 我只能看见她的眼睛。

examples.text: She has beautiful blue eyes.
examples.zh: 她有一双美丽的蓝眼睛。

vocabulary.definition: the thin layer of tissue that covers the bodies of humans and other animals

examples.text: She takes good care of her skin.
examples.zh: 她非常细心地保养自己的皮肤。

examples.text: You should protect your skin from the sun.
examples.zh: 您应该防止皮肤被太阳晒伤。

vocabulary.definition: the color of flamingos; red mixed with white

examples.text: She received a gift of pink roses.
examples.zh: 她收到的礼物是一束粉红色的玫瑰花。

examples.text: They all wore pink dresses at the wedding.
examples.ko: 그들은 그 결혼식에서 모두 분홍색 드레스를 입었습니다.
examples.zh: 她们在婚礼上都穿粉红色的裙子。

vocabulary.definition: to look good

examples.text: She looked very pretty in her wedding dress.
examples.ko: 그녀는 웨딩드레스 입은 모습이 매우 예뻤습니다.
examples.zh: 她穿婚纱的样子很漂亮。

examples.text: She has a very pretty face.
examples.ko: 그녀는 아주 예쁜 얼굴을 갖고 있습니다.
examples.zh: 她有一张非常漂亮的脸。

vocabulary.definition: the lightest color

examples.text: She's painting the room white.
examples.zh: 她在把这间房涂成白色。

examples.text: She's wearing a white shirt.
examples.zh: 她穿着一件白色衬衫。

vocabulary.definition: calm and free of worry

examples.text: After I came back from my vacation, I felt very relaxed.
examples.ko: 나의 휴가를 마치고 돌아온 후, 나는 매우 여유 있는 기분이 들었다.
examples.zh: 我度假归来感觉一身轻松。

examples.text: My colleague has a very relaxed style of working.
examples.ko: 나의 동료는 매우 느긋한 업무 방식을 가지고 있다.
examples.zh: 我同事的工作方式很轻松。

vocabulary.definition: to experience an emotion, or a physical sensation

examples.text: Getting a massage feels good.
examples.zh: 做按摩让人感觉很舒服。

examples.text: I feel much better today.
examples.zh: 我今天的感觉好多了。

vocabulary.definition: to move two things together in order to block an opening

examples.text: She's closing the door.
examples.zh: 她在关门。

examples.text: I can't close my suitcase.
examples.zh: 我关不上我的手提箱。

prompt: Describe the picture below. Check the Helpful Vocabulary section if you need help.

vocabulary.definition: glasses worn to protect the eyes from sunlight

examples.text: Don't forget to bring your sunglasses when we go to the beach.
examples.ko: 우리가 해변에 갈 때 너의 선글라스 가지고 오는 것을 잊지마.
examples.zh: 我們去海邊時，你不要忘了帶你的太陽眼鏡。

vocabulary.definition: a vehicle with four wheels that people drive to go from one place to another

examples.text: Where did I park my car?

vocabulary.definition: a person one knows well and enjoy spending time with

examples.text: These are my best friends.

vocabulary.definition: someone who drives a vehicle, typically as a job

examples.zh: 她给了出租车司机一大笔小费。

examples.text: She asked the driver to take her to the airport.

examples.zh: 她叫司机载她到机场。

vocabulary.definition: the seat beside the driver's seat in a car

examples.ko: 그 운전자의 아내는 조수석에서 깊이 잠들어 있었다.
examples.zh: 司機的妻子在副駕駛座上快速入睡。

vocabulary.definition: (often of a man) pleasant or appealing to look at; attractive

examples.zh: 我觉得他相当帅。

examples.text: He's not that handsome, but all the girls seem to love him.
examples.ko: 그는 그렇게 잘생긴 것은 아니지만, 모든 여자 아이들이 그를 매우 좋아하는 것처럼 보인다.
examples.zh: 他沒有很帥，但是所有的女孩都喜歡他。

vocabulary.definition: covering a great length or distance from end to end

examples.text: This bridge is very long.
examples.zh: 这座桥很长。

vocabulary.definition: to sit in and control a vehicle

examples.text: He loves driving to work.
examples.zh: 他喜欢开车上班。

examples.text: Please drive under the speed limit.
examples.zh: 请勿超过限速行驶。

vocabulary.definition: to raise the corners of one's mouth in order to show happiness or pleasure

examples.text: He's smiling at the camera.
examples.ko: 그는 카메라를 보고 미소를 짓고 있습니다.
examples.zh: 他正对着镜头笑呢。

examples.text: You look so happy when you smile.
examples.ko: 당신은 웃을 때 아주 행복해 보여요.
examples.zh: 你笑的时候看起来很开心。

vocabulary.definition: to move from one location to another

examples.text: I love traveling to different parts of the world.
examples.ko: 저는 세계 여러 나라를 여행하는 것을 좋아합니다.
examples.zh: 我喜欢到世界各地旅行。

examples.text: I traveled to London this summer with my boyfriend.

examples.zh: 今年夏天，我与男友去了伦敦旅游。

vocabulary.definition: to enjoy yourself during some activity

examples.text: We had a good time last night.
examples.zh: 我們昨晚玩得很開心。

examples.text: I don't think she's having a good time.
examples.ko: 그녀가 즐거운 시간을 보내고 있지 않은 것 같다.

vocabulary.definition: a female parent

examples.text: She's going to be a mother soon.

vocabulary.definition: a very young child, especially one that cannot talk or walk yet

examples.text: My baby is three months old.
examples.zh: 我的小孩有三个月大了。

examples.text: Your baby has the cutest smile.
examples.zh: 你宝宝的笑容太可爱了。

vocabulary.definition: the place where one lives permanently

examples.zh: 他们刚买了他们的第一幢房子。

vocabulary.definition: a cloth bag filled with soft material, placed under the head when sleeping

examples.zh: 她正要用枕头打她的丈夫。

vocabulary.definition: stationary organisms that require sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide for growth

vocabulary.definition: (of a woman or female animal) having a baby growing inside her body

vocabulary.definition: having the color of grass or leaves

examples.text: She has beautiful green eyes.
examples.zh: 她有一双迷人的绿眼睛。

vocabulary.definition: the color of flamingos; red mixed with white

examples.text: They all wore pink dresses at the wedding.

vocabulary.definition: feeling pleasure or joy; pleased

examples.text: He looks very happy.

examples.text: I have a happy family.

vocabulary.definition: providing comfort and relaxation

vocabulary.definition: to make physical contact with someone or something; to place one's hand on someone or something

examples.text: I care a lot about my dog.

vocabulary.definition: to raise the corners of one's mouth in order to show happiness or pleasure

examples.zh: 今天的水感觉不错。

vocabulary.definition: two glass lenses in a single frame that people wear in order to see better

examples.text: My daughter has to wear glasses from now on.
examples.ko: 이제부터 내 딸은 안경을 껴야 합니다.
examples.zh: 我女儿从现在起必须佩戴眼镜。

examples.text: I can't see well without my glasses.
examples.ko: 저는 안경을 쓰지 않으면 잘 보이지 않습니다.
examples.zh: 没有眼镜，我看得不太清楚。

vocabulary.definition: a piece of furniture with a large flat surface and four legs

examples.text: The table is set for dinner.
examples.zh: 晚餐桌子摆好了。

examples.text: Can you please clean the table?
examples.zh: 您能清理下饭桌吗？

vocabulary.definition: a document that shows how much you need to pay for something

examples.text: He is paying the bill with his credit card.
examples.ko: 그는 그의 신용 카드로 계산서를 결재하고 있다.
examples.zh: 他正在用信用卡买单。

examples.text: He left the hotel this morning after paying his bill.
examples.ko: 그는 청구서를 지급한 후 오늘 아침에 호텔을 떠났다.
examples.zh: 他今天早上付账后离开了宾馆。

vocabulary.definition: a situation or event that is hard to deal with; an issue or complication

examples.text: I have a problem with my car, so I will be late for work.
examples.ko: 차에 문제가 생겨 직장에 지각하게 되었습니다.
examples.zh: 我的车子出了问题，我上班可能会迟到。

examples.text: He has a big problem.
examples.zh: 他遇到了大麻烦。

vocabulary.definition: a pain felt in the head

examples.text: I've had a headache all day.
examples.zh: 我头痛了一整天。

examples.text: I have a headache again.
examples.zh: 我又头疼了。

vocabulary.definition: a portable computer that can be opened and closed like a book

examples.text: I need to get my laptop repaired.
examples.zh: 我需要把我的筆電拿去維修。

vocabulary.definition: nervous or anxious about real problems, or ones that could happen

examples.text: He looks very worried about his work.
examples.ko: 그는 그의 일에 대해 매우 걱정스러워하는 것으로 보인다.
examples.zh: 他似乎为自己的工作感到忧心忡忡。

examples.text: He's very worried about his father's surgery.
examples.ko: 그는 그의 아버지의 수술에 대해 매우 우려하고 있다.
examples.zh: 他非常担心父亲的手术。

vocabulary.definition: as much or as many as is needed

examples.text: This car has enough room for five passengers in the back seat.
examples.ko: 이 차량은 뒷좌석에 다섯 명의 승객을 위한 충분한 공간이 있다.
examples.zh: 这辆车的后座有足够的空间可以容纳五位乘客。

examples.text: There was enough food for at least thirty people.
examples.zh: 这些食物至少够三十人吃。

vocabulary.definition: to form ideas in one's mind

examples.text: He's thinking hard.
examples.zh: 他绞尽脑汁地想。

examples.text: What are you thinking about?
examples.zh: 你在想什么？

vocabulary.definition: look at and understand words

examples.text: He loves to read.
examples.zh: 他喜欢读书。

examples.text: His mother reads to him every day.
examples.ko: 그의 엄마는 매일 그에게 책을 읽어줍니다.
examples.zh: 他妈妈每天都会给他读故事。

vocabulary.definition: to have in one's hands or arms

examples.text: Can you hold these boxes for a minute?
examples.ko: 잠깐 동안 이 상자 좀 붙잡아 주시겠습니까?
examples.zh: 你可以把这些盒子抱一会儿吗？

examples.text: The mother is holding her baby.
examples.zh: 妈妈抱着婴儿。

vocabulary.definition: a female child

examples.text: His girl is two years old.
examples.zh: 他的女儿两岁。

examples.text: The girls are learning how to dance.
examples.zh: 这个女孩正在学习跳舞。

vocabulary.definition: an Italian dish that consists of a flat base of dough onto which tomato sauce, cheese, and other toppings are placed

examples.text: We went out for pizza last night.
examples.zh: 我們昨晚去吃披薩。

examples.text: Most children say that their favorite food is pizza.
examples.ko: 대부분의 아이들은 그들이 가장 좋아하는 음식이 피자라고 말한다.
examples.zh: 多數的小朋友說他們最愛的食物是披薩。

vocabulary.definition: the organ that allows animals and humans to see

examples.text: I can only see her eyes.
examples.zh: 我只能看见她的眼睛。

examples.text: She has beautiful blue eyes.
examples.zh: 她有一双美丽的蓝眼睛。

vocabulary.definition: the opening in the face that allows humans to eat, drink, and speak

examples.text: He's opening his mouth wide.
examples.zh: 他把嘴巴张得很大。

vocabulary.definition: a thin piece cut off from a larger whole

examples.text: That's the last slice of cake.
examples.zh: 那是最后一块蛋糕了。

examples.text: I cut a few thin slices of lime.
examples.zh: 我切了一些薄柠檬片。

vocabulary.definition: a solid dairy product that is often yellow or orange

examples.text: You can't make a pizza without cheese.
examples.ko: 치즈 없이는 피자를 만들 수 없다.
examples.zh: 你做披薩一定要配乳酪。

examples.text: Swiss cheese is full of holes.
examples.zh: 瑞士奶酪浑身都是孔。

vocabulary.definition: large; important or serious

examples.text: My dog is bigger than yours.
examples.ko: 우리 개가 당신 개보다 더 큽니다.
examples.zh: 我的狗比你的大。

examples.text: He has a big problem.
examples.zh: 他遇到了大麻烦。

vocabulary.definition: feeling pleasure or joy; pleased

examples.text: He looks very happy.
examples.zh: 他看上去非常高兴。

examples.text: I have a happy family.
examples.zh: 我有一个幸福的家。

vocabulary.definition: a group of people who play music together

examples.text: He's the lead member of a rock band.
There were almost fifty bands at the music festival.
examples.ko: 그 음악 축제에는 거의 50개의 밴드가 있었다.

vocabulary.definition: a road with buildings on either side of it

examples.text: The street is empty.
They are dancing in the street.

vocabulary.definition: a string instrument that is played by strumming or plucking the strings

examples.text: She is interested in playing the guitar.
examples.ko: 그녀는 기타 연주하는 것에 관심이 있다.

examples.text: Let's ask John to play something on his guitar!
examples.ko: 우리 John에게 그의 기타로 무엇인가 연주해달라고 요청하자!

vocabulary.definition: a musical instrument played by hitting with a hand or sticks

examples.text: He plays the drums in a band.
The drums help keep the rhythm of music.
examples.ko: 드럼은 음악의 박자를 유지하는 데 도움을 준다.

vocabulary.definition: a large low-pitched musical instrument
vocabulary.ko: 더블 베이스 (세워서 연주하는 네 줄 또는 다섯 줄의 현악기)

examples.text: He has played the double bass for five years now.
examples.ko: 그는 현재 5년 동안 더블 베이스를 연주해왔다.

vocabulary.definition: a type of clothing with a brim worn on the head

examples.text: She wore a hat to protect herself from the sun.
examples.ko: 그녀는 그녀 자신을 햇빛으로부터 보호하기 위해 모자를 썼다.

examples.text: My son loves wearing a cowboy hat.
examples.ko: 나의 아들은 카우보이 모자 쓰는 것을 좋아한다.

vocabulary.definition: a structure that has walls and a roof

examples.text: This building has a very original design.
examples.ko: 이 건물은 매우 독창적인 디자인을 하고 있다.

examples.text: That building is the post office.

vocabulary.definition: to make music on an instrument

examples.text: He likes to play tennis.
He's playing with his dad outside.
examples.ko: 그는 밖에서 그의 아버지와 놀고 있다.

vocabulary.definition: to be in a vertical position, supported by the feet

examples.text: He's standing at the top of the mountain.
They're standing in a long line.

vocabulary.definition: to have on the body as clothing or accessory

examples.text: She's wearing her mother's shoes.
examples.ko: 그녀는 그녀의 어머니의 신발을 신고 있다.

examples.text: She likes wearing leather.

vocabulary.definition: a room where people gather to watch TV, talk, etc.

examples.text: We have a big table and a sofa in our living room.
examples.ko: 우리는 우리 거실에 큰 식탁과 소파를 두고 있다.

examples.text: Everyone in the living room is waiting for you.

vocabulary.definition: a device that broadcasts video

examples.text: I think you watch too much television.
examples.ko: 나는 네가 텔레비전을 너무 많이 본다고 생각해.

examples.text: We watched the game on television last night.
examples.ko: 우리는 어젯밤에 그 경기를 텔레비전으로 봤다.

vocabulary.definition: a soft piece of furniture that can seat at least two people

examples.text: The sofa is in the corner of the room.
My dog likes to sleep on the sofa.
examples.ko: 나의 개는 소파 위에서 자기를 좋아한다.

vocabulary.definition: a living thing that grows in soil and has a stem, branches, leaves and/or flowers

examples.text: My wife bought some plants for our balcony.
examples.ko: 나의 아내는 우리 발코니를 위한 식물을 조금 샀다.

examples.text: We have some plants in our bedroom.
examples.ko: 우리는 우리의 침실에 식물을 조금 가지고 있다.

vocabulary.definition: large in size

examples.text: My dog is bigger than yours.
examples.ko: 나의 개가 너의 개보다 더 크다.

examples.text: He has a big problem.

vocabulary.definition: costing a lot of money

examples.text: Do you have shirts that are not so expensive?
This is a very expensive restaurant.

vocabulary.definition: arranged neatly and well ordered

examples.text: She likes to keep her house clean and tidy.
examples.ko: 그녀는 그녀의 집을 깨끗하고 잘 정돈된 상태로 유지하기를 좋아한다.

examples.text: My roommate always keeps her room tidy.
examples.ko: 나의 룸메이트는 항상 그녀의 방을 깔끔하게 유지한다.

vocabulary.definition: an alcoholic drink made from grapes

examples.text: We had some excellent red wine with lunch.
examples.ko: 우리는 점심 식사와 함께 훌륭한 레드와인을 조금 마셨다

examples.text: Can you pour me a glass of wine?
examples.ko: 와인 한 잔을 따라주실 수 있나요?

vocabulary.definition: a container that has a long, narrow neck and a wide body, usually made from glass or plastic

examples.text: Let's buy a bottle of wine for dinner tonight.
examples.ko: 우리 오늘 저녁 식사를 위해 와인을 한 병 사자.

examples.text: I try to drink at least one bottle of water a day.
examples.ko: 나는 하루에 최소한 한 병의 물을 마시려고 노력한다.

vocabulary.definition: a sharp tool used for cutting things

examples.text: Can I have a clean knife, please?
examples.ko: 부탁인데, 깨끗한 나이프를 줄 수 있나요?

examples.text: He's cutting the tomatoes with a sharp knife.
examples.ko: 그는 토마토를 날카로운 칼로 자르고 있다.

vocabulary.definition: a hard, often yellow dairy product

examples.text: Swiss cheese is full of holes.
I had a ham and cheese sandwich for lunch.
examples.ko: 나는 점심으로 햄과 치즈 샌드위치를 먹었다.

vocabulary.definition: a piece of furniture that consists of a large, flat surface and four legs

examples.text: The table is set for dinner.
Can you please clean the table?

vocabulary.definition: a plant used to make the taste of food better

examples.text: The cook is adding some herbs to the dish.
examples.ko: 요리사가 요리에 허브를 약간 첨가하고 있다.

vocabulary.definition: having the color of snow

examples.text: She's painting the room white.
examples.ko: 그녀는 그 방을 흰색으로 페인트칠하고 있다.

examples.text: She's wearing a white shirt.

vocabulary.definition: having the color of lemons

examples.text: My husband bought me a dozen yellow roses.
examples.ko: 나의 남편이 나를 위해 노란색 장미를 12송이 샀다.

examples.text: She's wearing a yellow kimono.
